test.observation: Jest ran 38 suites / 360 tests and all passed. Output still included the expected test-exercised agent-store/agent-init console logging and the wallet-connect error-path console.error.

typecheck.observation: tsc --noEmit completed successfully with no TypeScript diagnostics.

lint.observation: ESLint exited 0 but reported 84 warnings, primarily pre-existing no-bitwise, no-void, and eslint-comments/no-unused-disable findings across tests and a few screens/stores.

blockingIssues.description: `hydrateRestored()` still mutates the route-driving session flags to the ready state before awaiting the durable `SESSION_KEY` write, while AppNavigator derives gates declaratively from those flags. A SecureStorage write failure can therefore still move the app off RecoveryRestore before the rejection path can keep the user in place.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance that onboarding/restore navigation is declarative from `session-store` flags, so validators and workers must verify gate-changing flows through `AppNavigator` or integration coverage rather than relying only on screen-level callback assertions like `onRestored` or `navigation.reset`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The round-4 review for `fix-restore-session-persistence-propagate-errors` found that even after the screen skipped `onRestored()` on persistence failure, `hydrateRestored()` still flipped `biometricStatus` / `hasCompletedOnboarding` / `hasIdentity` / `isLocked`, and `AppNavigator` immediately re-routed from those fields (`src/navigation/app-navigator.tsx`, `src/features/session/get-initial-route.ts`). The isolated screen rejection test passed while the real declarative navigation bug remained.
